storyTitle: As Operations Manager, I want to perform module-level performance benchmarking and comparison to achieve identification of optimization opportunities across insurance operations

functional.title: Verify benchmark report displays all insurance modules ranked by response time against defined targets
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
At least 5 insurance modules (e.g., Claims, Underwriting, Policy Admin, Billing, Renewals) have recorded performance data in the production environment
Performance targets (response time, throughput, error rate) have been configured for each module in the system settings
User is on the main Operations Dashboard page

steps.action: Click on the 'Performance Benchmarking' menu item in the left navigation panel
steps.expectedResult: The Performance Benchmarking landing page loads displaying a summary overview with options for 'Benchmark Reports', 'Optimization Recommendations', and 'Environment Comparison'

steps.action: Click the 'Benchmark Reports' card or tab
steps.expectedResult: The Benchmark Reports page loads showing a table/grid of all insurance modules with columns: Module Name, Avg Response Time, Target Response Time, Throughput (req/s), Target Throughput, Error Rate (%), Target Error Rate, and Status (Pass/Fail indicator)

steps.action: Verify the default sort order of the modules in the report table
steps.expectedResult: All insurance modules are ranked by response time in descending order (slowest first), clearly identifying the slowest operations at the top of the list

steps.action: Click the column header 'Throughput (req/s)' to re-sort the table
steps.expectedResult: The table re-sorts all modules by throughput, with the lowest throughput modules appearing first. A sort indicator arrow appears on the Throughput column header

steps.action: Click the column header 'Error Rate (%)' to re-sort the table
steps.expectedResult: The table re-sorts all modules by error rate in descending order (highest error rate first). Each module row shows a red indicator if the error rate exceeds the defined target, and green if within target

steps.action: Verify that each module row displays a comparison of actual vs. target values for all three metrics
steps.expectedResult: Each row shows actual values alongside target values for response time, throughput, and error rate. Modules exceeding targets are visually highlighted (e.g., red text or warning icon), and modules meeting targets show a green checkmark or 'Pass' status

postconditions: Benchmark report is displayed with all insurance modules ranked correctly by the selected metric
User remains on the Benchmark Reports page and can continue interacting with the report
No data has been modified; the report is read-only
Report generation event is logged in the system audit trail with timestamp and user ID

functional.title: Verify benchmark report identifies and ranks top operations within each module by response time
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
The Claims module has at least 10 distinct operations (e.g., Create Claim, Approve Claim, Reject Claim, Search Claims, etc.) with recorded performance data
Performance targets are defined at the operation level for the Claims module
User is on the Benchmark Reports page

steps.action: Click on the 'Claims' module row in the benchmark report table to expand or drill down into module-level details
steps.expectedResult: A detailed view opens showing all operations within the Claims module, listed in a sub-table with columns: Operation Name, Avg Response Time (ms), Target Response Time (ms), Throughput, Target Throughput, Error Rate, Target Error Rate, and Variance (%)

steps.action: Verify the operations are ranked by response time in descending order (slowest first)
steps.expectedResult: The top operations are listed with the slowest response times at the top. For example, 'Generate Claim Report' at 4500ms appears above 'Search Claims' at 2100ms

steps.action: Verify the Variance column shows the percentage difference between actual and target for each operation
steps.expectedResult: Each operation row displays a calculated variance percentage. Operations exceeding targets show a positive variance in red (e.g., '+125%' meaning 125% over target), while operations within target show a negative or zero variance in green

steps.action: Click the 'Back to Module Overview' breadcrumb or back button
steps.expectedResult: User returns to the main Benchmark Reports page showing all modules ranked, with the previous sort order and filters preserved

postconditions: Drill-down into module operations displayed correct ranking of top operations by response time
User is back on the main Benchmark Reports overview page
No data modifications occurred during the drill-down

functional.title: Verify optimization recommendations are generated for operations exceeding target thresholds
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
At least 3 operations across different modules have actual performance metrics exceeding their defined target thresholds (e.g., Claims > Create Claim response time is 3000ms vs. target 1500ms)
The recommendation engine has been configured with optimization rules and estimated impact calculations
User is on the Performance Benchmarking landing page

steps.action: Click on the 'Optimization Recommendations' tab or card on the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: The Optimization Recommendations page loads displaying a list of recommendations, each tied to a specific operation that exceeds its target threshold

steps.action: Verify the first recommendation entry contains the required fields: Module Name, Operation Name, Current Metric Value, Target Value, Recommendation Description, and Estimated Improvement Impact
steps.expectedResult: The recommendation displays all required fields. For example: Module: 'Claims', Operation: 'Create Claim', Current Response Time: '3000ms', Target: '1500ms', Recommendation: 'Optimize database query for claim creation by adding index on policy_id', Estimated Impact: 'Reduce response time by ~40% (to ~1800ms)'

steps.action: Verify that recommendations are sorted by estimated improvement impact in descending order (highest impact first)
steps.expectedResult: Recommendations with the highest estimated improvement impact appear at the top of the list, allowing the Operations Manager to prioritize the most impactful optimizations

steps.action: Click on a specific recommendation row to view its detailed breakdown
steps.expectedResult: A detail panel or modal opens showing: full recommendation description, root cause analysis summary, estimated effort level (Low/Medium/High), estimated improvement percentage, historical trend of the metric, and a link to the related benchmark report entry

steps.action: Click the 'Export Recommendations' button in the top-right corner of the Optimization Recommendations page
steps.expectedResult: A file download dialog appears offering export in CSV or PDF format. The exported file contains all displayed recommendations with their full details including module, operation, current values, targets, recommendations, and estimated impacts

postconditions: Optimization recommendations are displayed for all operations exceeding target thresholds
Each recommendation includes an estimated improvement impact value
Export file (if downloaded) is saved to the user's local machine with correct data
System audit log records the recommendation report access and any export actions

functional.title: Verify benchmark comparison across environments (dev, staging, production) identifies environment-specific variances
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager with access to all three environments (dev, staging, production)
Performance benchmark data exists for the same set of modules and operations across dev, staging, and production environments
Environment configurations are registered in the system (e.g., dev: 2 CPU/4GB RAM, staging: 4 CPU/8GB RAM, production: 8 CPU/16GB RAM)
User is on the Performance Benchmarking landing page

steps.action: Click on the 'Environment Comparison' tab or card on the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: The Environment Comparison page loads displaying a multi-environment comparison view with environment selector checkboxes for 'Dev', 'Staging', and 'Production', all checked by default

steps.action: Verify the comparison table displays all modules with performance metrics side-by-side for each environment
steps.expectedResult: A comparison table is displayed with columns grouped by environment: Dev (Response Time, Throughput, Error Rate), Staging (Response Time, Throughput, Error Rate), Production (Response Time, Throughput, Error Rate). Each module row shows actual values for all three environments

steps.action: Verify that environment-specific performance variances are highlighted in the comparison table
steps.expectedResult: Cells where one environment significantly deviates from others are highlighted. For example, if the Claims module response time is 500ms in dev, 600ms in staging, but 2500ms in production, the production cell is highlighted in red/orange with a variance indicator showing '+316% vs. dev'

steps.action: Uncheck the 'Dev' environment checkbox to filter the comparison to only Staging and Production
steps.expectedResult: The comparison table updates to show only Staging and Production columns. The variance calculations update to compare only between the two selected environments

steps.action: Click on a highlighted variance cell (e.g., Production Claims response time) to view the variance detail
steps.expectedResult: A detail panel opens showing: environment configuration differences, historical performance trend for that operation in each environment, potential causes for the variance (e.g., 'Production has 10x more concurrent users'), and a link to related optimization recommendations

steps.action: Re-check the 'Dev' checkbox and click the 'Generate Variance Report' button
steps.expectedResult: A variance summary report is generated showing all environment-specific variances ranked by severity, with the most significant variances listed first. The report includes a summary section stating total number of variances found and the top 3 most critical ones

postconditions: Environment comparison view correctly displays side-by-side metrics for all selected environments
Environment-specific variances are identified and highlighted with percentage differences
Variance report is generated and available for review or export
User remains on the Environment Comparison page with all filters intact

functional.title: Verify date range filtering on benchmark reports returns correct time-scoped data
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
Performance benchmark data exists for at least the last 90 days across all modules
User is on the Benchmark Reports page
Default date range is set to 'Last 30 Days'

steps.action: Verify the default date range filter shows 'Last 30 Days' and the report data corresponds to the last 30 days
steps.expectedResult: The date range picker displays 'Last 30 Days' as the selected option. The report header or subtitle shows the exact date range (e.g., 'May 16, 2025 - June 15, 2025'). All metric values in the table are aggregated from data within this 30-day window

steps.action: Click the date range picker and select 'Last 7 Days'
steps.expectedResult: The report refreshes and displays benchmark data aggregated over the last 7 days only. Module rankings may change as the shorter time window may show different performance patterns. A loading indicator appears briefly during data refresh

steps.action: Click the date range picker and select 'Custom Range', then enter start date '2025-04-01' and end date '2025-04-30'
steps.expectedResult: The report refreshes to show benchmark data for April 2025 only. The report header updates to show 'April 1, 2025 - April 30, 2025'. All metrics reflect the custom date range

steps.action: Compare the module rankings between the 'Last 7 Days' view and the 'Custom Range (April 2025)' view by noting the top 3 slowest modules in each
steps.expectedResult: The rankings may differ between time periods, demonstrating that the system correctly scopes data to the selected date range. The data is not stale or cached from a previous query

postconditions: Benchmark report correctly reflects data within the selected date range
Date range filter persists on the page until the user changes it or navigates away
No data outside the selected date range is included in the report calculations

functional.title: Verify API endpoint returns correct benchmark data with proper authentication
preconditions: A valid API authentication token exists for an Operations Manager user
Performance benchmark data is available in the application database for at least 3 modules
API endpoint URL is documented (e.g., GET /api/v1/benchmarks/reports)
API testing tool (e.g., Postman or curl) is available

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/reports' with a valid Authorization header (Bearer token) and query parameter 'environment=production'
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON response body containing an array of module benchmark objects. Each object includes: moduleId, moduleName, avgResponseTime, targetResponseTime, throughput, targetThroughput, errorRate, targetErrorRate, status, and timestamp

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/reports' without an Authorization header
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with an error message: {"error": "Authentication required", "message": "A valid authentication token must be provided"}

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/recommendations?environment=production' with a valid Authorization header
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON response body containing an array of optimization recommendation objects. Each object includes: recommendationId, moduleId, operationName, currentValue, targetValue, recommendation, estimatedImpact, and priority

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/compare?environments=dev,staging,production' with a valid Authorization header
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON response body containing environment comparison data structured by module, with nested objects for each environment showing their respective metrics and calculated variances

steps.action: Verify the response time of the API call is within SLA requirements (e.g., under 3 seconds)
steps.expectedResult: The API response is received within the defined SLA threshold. Response headers include appropriate cache-control directives and content-type is 'application/json'

postconditions: API endpoints return correct, well-structured JSON data matching the UI display
Authentication is enforced on all benchmark API endpoints
API response times meet SLA requirements
No sensitive data is leaked in error responses for unauthenticated requests

functional.title: Verify system correctly processes and returns results when benchmark data is refreshed (Scenario 4)
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
Benchmark data was last refreshed more than 1 hour ago
New performance data has been ingested into the application database since the last refresh
User is on the Benchmark Reports page viewing stale data

steps.action: Note the 'Last Updated' timestamp displayed on the Benchmark Reports page header (e.g., 'Last Updated: June 15, 2025 08:00 AM')
steps.expectedResult: The 'Last Updated' timestamp is visible and shows the time of the last data refresh, which is more than 1 hour ago

steps.action: Click the 'Refresh Data' button (or equivalent refresh icon) on the Benchmark Reports page
steps.expectedResult: A loading spinner or progress indicator appears. The system begins processing the latest performance data from the application database. A status message such as 'Refreshing benchmark data...' is displayed

steps.action: Wait for the refresh operation to complete
steps.expectedResult: The loading indicator disappears. The 'Last Updated' timestamp updates to the current time (e.g., 'Last Updated: June 15, 2025 10:30 AM'). A success notification appears: 'Benchmark data refreshed successfully'

steps.action: Verify the benchmark report data has been updated with the latest metrics
steps.expectedResult: Module rankings and metric values reflect the newly ingested data. If a module's performance changed (e.g., Claims response time improved from 3000ms to 2200ms), the updated value is displayed and the ranking order may have changed accordingly

steps.action: Navigate to the Optimization Recommendations page and verify recommendations are updated based on the refreshed data
steps.expectedResult: Recommendations reflect the latest benchmark data. If an operation that previously exceeded its target now meets the target after the refresh, its recommendation is either removed or marked as 'Resolved'. New recommendations may appear for operations that now exceed targets

postconditions: Benchmark data is refreshed and persisted in the database with the latest performance metrics
All dependent views (reports, recommendations, comparisons) reflect the updated data
The 'Last Updated' timestamp accurately reflects the most recent refresh time
System audit log records the data refresh event with user ID and timestamp

functional.title: Verify system handles concurrent benchmark report access and returns expected results (Scenario 5)
preconditions: Two separate Operations Manager user accounts are available (UserA and UserB)
Both users have 'benchmark_reports' permission
Performance benchmark data exists for all modules in the production environment
Both users are logged in on separate browser sessions

steps.action: As UserA, navigate to the Performance Benchmarking page and click 'Benchmark Reports'
steps.expectedResult: UserA sees the full benchmark report with all modules ranked by response time. The report loads within the SLA time limit

steps.action: Simultaneously, as UserB, navigate to the Performance Benchmarking page and click 'Benchmark Reports'
steps.expectedResult: UserB also sees the full benchmark report with identical data to UserA. Both sessions load independently without interference or data corruption

steps.action: As UserA, apply a date range filter of 'Last 7 Days' on the benchmark report
steps.expectedResult: UserA's report updates to show data for the last 7 days only. UserB's report remains unchanged (still showing the default 'Last 30 Days' view)

steps.action: As UserB, navigate to the Environment Comparison page and select only 'Staging' and 'Production' environments
steps.expectedResult: UserB sees the environment comparison for Staging and Production. UserA's session is unaffected and still shows the filtered benchmark report

steps.action: As UserA, click 'Refresh Data' on the benchmark report
steps.expectedResult: UserA's data refreshes successfully. UserB's current view is not disrupted. If UserB refreshes their page afterward, they will see the updated data as well

postconditions: Both users received correct and consistent benchmark data without cross-session interference
Filters and selections applied by one user do not affect the other user's session
System handled concurrent access without errors, data corruption, or performance degradation
Database integrity is maintained with no duplicate or conflicting records

functional.title: Verify benchmark report visual indicators correctly distinguish pass/fail status against targets
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
At least 2 modules are within target thresholds (pass) and at least 2 modules exceed target thresholds (fail)
User is on the Benchmark Reports page

steps.action: Observe the status column for a module that meets all three targets (response time, throughput, error rate)
steps.expectedResult: The module row displays a green checkmark icon or 'Pass' badge in the Status column. The response time, throughput, and error rate cells are displayed in standard or green text

steps.action: Observe the status column for a module that exceeds the response time target but meets throughput and error rate targets
steps.expectedResult: The module row displays a yellow/amber warning icon or 'Partial' badge in the Status column. The response time cell is highlighted in red/orange, while throughput and error rate cells remain in standard/green text

steps.action: Observe the status column for a module that exceeds all three target thresholds
steps.expectedResult: The module row displays a red X icon or 'Fail' badge in the Status column. All three metric cells (response time, throughput, error rate) are highlighted in red

steps.action: Hover over a red-highlighted metric cell to view the tooltip
steps.expectedResult: A tooltip appears showing: 'Current: 3000ms | Target: 1500ms | Variance: +100%' (with actual values for the specific metric). The tooltip provides clear context for the failure

postconditions: Visual indicators correctly reflect pass/fail/partial status for all modules
Color coding is consistent across the entire report
Tooltips provide additional context for all highlighted metrics

functional.title: Verify benchmark report data persistence and retrieval after user session ends
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
A benchmark report has been generated and viewed with specific filters (date range: 'Last 7 Days', sorted by Error Rate)
User is on the Benchmark Reports page

steps.action: Note the current benchmark report data, including the top 3 modules by error rate and their exact metric values
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark report displays the current data with the applied filters. User records the top 3 modules and their error rates for later comparison

steps.action: Log out of the application by clicking the user avatar in the top-right corner and selecting 'Sign Out'
steps.expectedResult: User is redirected to the login page. Session is terminated

steps.action: Log back in with the same Operations Manager credentials and navigate to the Performance Benchmarking > Benchmark Reports page
steps.expectedResult: The Benchmark Reports page loads successfully. The report displays the default view (Last 30 Days, sorted by response time) since session-specific filters are not persisted across sessions

steps.action: Re-apply the same filters (date range: 'Last 7 Days', sort by Error Rate) and compare the data with the previously noted values
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark data matches the previously noted values exactly (assuming no new data was ingested during the brief logout period). This confirms that the underlying benchmark data is persisted in the database and not lost when the session ends

postconditions: Benchmark data is correctly persisted in the application database independent of user sessions
Data retrieval after re-login returns consistent and accurate results
User session filters are reset to defaults upon new login (expected behavior)

functional.title: Verify Operations Manager can export benchmark report in multiple formats
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
Benchmark report is displayed with data for at least 5 modules
User is on the Benchmark Reports page with default filters applied
Browser allows file downloads

steps.action: Click the 'Export' button located in the top-right area of the Benchmark Reports page
steps.expectedResult: A dropdown menu appears with export format options: 'Export as CSV', 'Export as PDF', and optionally 'Export as Excel'

steps.action: Select 'Export as CSV' from the dropdown
steps.expectedResult: A CSV file is downloaded to the user's default download location. The filename includes the report type and date (e.g., 'benchmark_report_2025-06-15.csv'). A success toast notification appears: 'Report exported successfully'

steps.action: Open the downloaded CSV file and verify its contents
steps.expectedResult: The CSV file contains headers matching the report columns: Module Name, Avg Response Time, Target Response Time, Throughput, Target Throughput, Error Rate, Target Error Rate, Status. All module rows from the report are present with correct values matching the UI display

steps.action: Click the 'Export' button again and select 'Export as PDF'
steps.expectedResult: A PDF file is downloaded with a formatted version of the benchmark report including the report title, date range, generation timestamp, and a properly formatted table of all modules with their metrics. The PDF includes visual indicators (color coding) for pass/fail status

postconditions: Both CSV and PDF export files are saved to the user's local machine
Exported data matches the on-screen report data exactly
Export action is logged in the system audit trail
No data corruption or truncation in exported files

functional.title: Verify role-based access control restricts benchmark features to authorized users only
preconditions: Three user accounts exist: Operations Manager (full access), Read-Only Analyst (view-only access), and Claims Adjuster (no benchmark access)
All three users are able to log in to the application
Performance benchmark data exists in the system

steps.action: Log in as the Operations Manager and navigate to the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: The Performance Benchmarking page loads fully with all features accessible: Benchmark Reports, Optimization Recommendations, Environment Comparison, Refresh Data button, and Export button are all visible and enabled

steps.action: Log out and log in as the Read-Only Analyst. Navigate to the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: The Performance Benchmarking page loads with view-only access. Benchmark Reports and Environment Comparison are visible and viewable. The 'Refresh Data' button is either hidden or disabled (greyed out). Export functionality may be available for read-only users

steps.action: Log out and log in as the Claims Adjuster. Attempt to navigate to the Performance Benchmarking page via direct URL '/performance-benchmarking'
steps.expectedResult: The user is either redirected to an 'Access Denied' page with message 'You do not have permission to access Performance Benchmarking' or the Performance Benchmarking menu item is not visible in the navigation at all

steps.action: As the Claims Adjuster, attempt to access the benchmark API directly: GET '/api/v1/benchmarks/reports' with the Claims Adjuster's auth token
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden with error message: {"error": "Access denied", "message": "You do not have the required permissions to access benchmark reports"}

postconditions: Operations Manager has full read/write access to all benchmark features
Read-Only Analyst can view reports but cannot trigger data refresh or modifications
Claims Adjuster is completely blocked from accessing benchmark features via both UI and API
Access control violations are logged in the security audit trail

functional.title: Verify benchmark report displays throughput metrics correctly with proper units and formatting
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager

Modules have varying throughput levels: high throughput (e.g., Policy Search at 500 req/s), medium throughput (e.g., Claims Processing at 50 req/s), and low throughput (e.g., Report Generation at 2 req/s)
Throughput targets are defined for each module
User is on the Benchmark Reports page

steps.action: Verify the Throughput column displays values with appropriate units (e.g., 'req/s' or 'requests/second')
steps.expectedResult: All throughput values are displayed with consistent units. High throughput modules show values like '500 req/s', medium modules show '50 req/s', and low throughput modules show '2 req/s'. Units are clearly labeled in the column header or next to each value

steps.action: Click the 'Throughput' column header to sort modules by throughput in ascending order (lowest first)
steps.expectedResult: Modules are re-sorted with the lowest throughput at the top (e.g., Report Generation at 2 req/s) and highest at the bottom (e.g., Policy Search at 500 req/s). Modules with throughput below their target are highlighted

steps.action: Verify that modules with throughput below their target are flagged appropriately
steps.expectedResult: Modules where actual throughput is below the target throughput are highlighted with a red/warning indicator. For example, if Claims Processing target is 100 req/s but actual is 50 req/s, the cell shows a red highlight and the variance is displayed as '-50%'

postconditions: Throughput metrics are displayed with correct values, units, and formatting
Sorting by throughput works correctly in both ascending and descending order
Below-target throughput values are visually distinguished from on-target values

functional.title: Verify environment comparison chart/visualization renders correctly for all three environments
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager
Performance data exists for dev, staging, and production environments for at least 5 common modules
User is on the Environment Comparison page

steps.action: Verify that a visual chart (bar chart, line chart, or radar chart) is displayed alongside the comparison table showing environment performance side-by-side
steps.expectedResult: A chart visualization is rendered showing performance metrics for all three environments. Each environment is represented by a distinct color (e.g., Dev=blue, Staging=yellow, Production=green). A legend clearly identifies each environment

steps.action: Hover over a data point on the chart for the 'Claims' module in the 'Production' environment
steps.expectedResult: A tooltip appears showing: 'Claims - Production: Response Time: 2500ms, Throughput: 50 req/s, Error Rate: 2.5%'. The tooltip data matches the corresponding row in the comparison table

steps.action: Toggle the chart metric selector from 'Response Time' to 'Error Rate'
steps.expectedResult: The chart updates to display error rate comparisons across environments. The Y-axis label changes to 'Error Rate (%)' and the data points reflect error rate values for each module across all environments

steps.action: Uncheck the 'Dev' environment from the chart legend or filter
steps.expectedResult: The Dev environment data is removed from the chart. Only Staging and Production data points remain. The chart rescales appropriately to the remaining data range

postconditions: Chart visualization accurately represents the benchmark comparison data
Interactive elements (tooltips, filters, metric selectors) function correctly
Chart data is consistent with the tabular comparison data

functional.title: Verify input validation on benchmark target configuration prevents invalid values
preconditions: User is logged in as an Operations Manager with admin-level permissions to configure benchmark targets
User navigates to the Benchmark Configuration or Settings page where performance targets are defined
At least one module (e.g., Claims) has existing target values configured

steps.action: Click 'Edit' on the Claims module target configuration row
steps.expectedResult: An edit form or inline editor opens showing current target values: Response Time Target (ms), Throughput Target (req/s), Error Rate Target (%). All fields are editable

steps.action: Clear the 'Response Time Target' field and enter a negative value: '-500'
steps.expectedResult: A validation error message appears immediately: 'Response time target must be a positive number'. The 'Save' button is disabled or the form prevents submission

steps.action: Enter a valid response time target of '2000', enter a valid throughput target of '100', and enter an error rate target of '150' (exceeding 100%)
steps.expectedResult: A validation error appears on the Error Rate Target field: 'Error rate target must be between 0 and 100%'. The form prevents saving until the value is corrected

steps.action: Correct the error rate target to '5', and click the 'Save' button
steps.expectedResult: A success notification appears: 'Benchmark targets for Claims module updated successfully'. The configuration page refreshes showing the updated target values: Response Time: 2000ms, Throughput: 100 req/s, Error Rate: 5%

steps.action: Navigate to the Benchmark Reports page and verify the Claims module now uses the updated targets
steps.expectedResult: The Claims module row in the benchmark report shows the updated target values (2000ms, 100 req/s, 5%). The pass/fail status is recalculated based on the new targets

postconditions: Updated benchmark targets are persisted in the database
Benchmark reports reflect the newly configured targets immediately
Invalid inputs are rejected with clear, user-friendly error messages
Configuration change is logged in the audit trail with old and new values

userStoryObject.title: As Operations Manager, I want to perform module-level performance benchmarking and comparison to achieve identification of optimization opportunities across insurance operations
userStoryObject.description:
CONTEXT & BACKGROUND:
Benchmark reports must compare actual performance against targets for each insurance module, identifying the slowest operations and highest-impact optimization opportunities.

CURRENT STATE:
Manual or incomplete process today.

DESIRED STATE:
System supports the capability end-to-end.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
• Implement core logic and validation
• Expose API and UI
• Handle errors and edge cases
• Ensure data persistence and retrieval

USER INTERACTION FLOW:
1. User accesses the feature
2. User performs the required action
3. System processes the request
4. System displays results or confirmation
5. User verifies the outcome

TECHNICAL CONSIDERATIONS:
• Data source: application database
• Security: authentication and input validation
• Performance: meet SLA requirements

OUT OF SCOPE:
• Advanced customization (future story)

SUCCESS METRICS:
• Feature works per acceptance criteria
• No critical defects in production

**Persona:** Operations Manager

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Benchmark reports rank all insurance modules and top operations by response time, throughput, and error rate against defined targets
2. Optimization recommendations are generated for operations exceeding target thresholds with estimated improvement impact
3. Benchmark comparisons across environments (dev, staging, production) identify environment-specific performance variances
4. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 4
5. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 5
